---
title: "B | Daten mit `lavaan` erzeugen"
format: live-html
engine: knitr  
status: "Version 0.1"
lang: de 
webr:
  cell-options:
    timelimit: 600
---

{{< include _extensions/r-wasm/live/_knitr.qmd >}}
 

```{webr}
#| warning: false
#| echo: false

webr::install("quadprog",  repos = "https://kroehne.github.io/webr-quadprog-repo")
 
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(mnormt))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(lavaan))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(lavaanPlot))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(DT))

assignInNamespace("lav_options_checkinterval", function(x, nm, num2int) {
    if (any(is.na(x))) return(TRUE)
    if (num2int) x <- as.integer(x)                                                                                              
    oks <- vapply(x, function(x1) {
      (x1 > nm$bounds[1] || (x1 == nm$bounds[1] && nm$first.in)) &&                                                              
      (x1 < nm$bounds[2] || (x1 == nm$bounds[2] && nm$last.in))                                                                  
    }, TRUE)
    all(oks)                                                                                                                     
  }, ns = "lavaan")
  
View <- function(dat){
  dat_name <- deparse(substitute(dat))
  df_display <- dat |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    dplyr::across(everything(), ~ ifelse(is.na(.x), "❌ (NA)", .x))
  )
  
  DT::datatable(
    df_display,
    rownames = FALSE, 
    options = list(
      pageLength = 5,
      scrollX = TRUE,
      dom = 'tip',   
      columnDefs = list(list(className = 'dt-center', targets = "_all"))
    ),
    caption = htmltools::tags$caption(
      style = 'caption-side: top; text-align: left;',
      'data.frame: ', htmltools::strong(dat_name)
    )
  )  
}
```


## Beispiel 

### Daten aus einem SEM-Modell erzeugen

```{webr}
#| warning: false
#| echo: true
#| autorun: true
# Populationsmodell definieren
pop_model <- '
  F1 =~ 0.8*x1 + 0.7*x2 + 0.6*x3
  F2 =~ 0.8*x4 + 0.7*x5 + 0.6*x6
  F1 ~~ 0.3*F2
  F1 ~~ 1*F1; F2 ~~ 1*F2
  x1 ~~ 0.36*x1; x2 ~~ 0.51*x2
  x3 ~~ 0.64*x3; x4 ~~ 0.36*x4
  x5 ~~ 0.51*x5; x6 ~~ 0.64*x6
'

# Daten simulieren
set.seed(2026)
sim_data <- simulateData(pop_model, sample.nobs = 200)
head(sim_data, 3)

```

### Analyse

```{webr}
#| warning: false
#| echo: true
#| autorun: false
# CFA auf simulierte Daten
fit_sim <- cfa('
  F1 =~ x1 + x2 + x3
  F2 =~ x4 + x5 + x6',
  data = sim_data)

cat("Wahre Korrelation: 0.30\n")
cat("Geschätzt:",
  round(lavInspect(fit_sim, "cor.lv")[1,2], 3))
```

## Poweranalyse

::: {.callout-caution}
Achtung! Das läuft im Browser sehr lange. Am besten in R lokal ausführen!
:::

```{webr}
#| warning: false
#| echo: true
#| autorun: false
set.seed(2026)

# --- Einstellungen ---
n_rep   <- 250          # Anzahl Replikationen (sollte > 1000 sein)
N       <- 200          # Stichprobengröße
alpha   <- 0.05         # Signifikanzniveau
true_cor <- 0.3         # Wahre latente Korrelation

# Populationsmodell 
pop_model <- paste0('
  F1 =~ 0.8*x1 + 0.7*x2 + 0.6*x3
  F2 =~ 0.8*x4 + 0.7*x5 + 0.6*x6
  F1 ~~ ',true_cor,'*F2
  F1 ~~ 1*F1; F2 ~~ 1*F2
  x1 ~~ 0.36*x1; x2 ~~ 0.51*x2
  x3 ~~ 0.64*x3; x4 ~~ 0.36*x4
  x5 ~~ 0.51*x5; x6 ~~ 0.64*x6
')

# Analysemodell
ana_model <- '
  F1 =~ x1 + x2 + x3
  F2 =~ x4 + x5 + x6
'

# --- Simulation ---
sig <- logical(n_rep)

for (i in seq_len(n_rep)) {
  d <- simulateData(pop_model, sample.nobs = N)
  fit <- cfa(ana_model, data = d,
             warn = FALSE, se = "standard")
  
  # Parameterzeile für F1 ~~ F2 extrahieren
  pe <- parameterEstimates(fit)
  row <- pe[pe$lhs == "F1" & pe$op == "~~" & pe$rhs == "F2", ]
  
  sig[i] <- row$pvalue < alpha
}

# --- Ergebnis ---
power <- mean(sig)
cat(sprintf(
  "Empirische Power (N=%d, r=%.2f, alpha=%.2f, %d Replikationen):\n  %.1f %%\n",
  N, true_cor, alpha, n_rep, power * 100))
```
